profiles.name: Official log-log formula fit through published 1/10/100 sec rows

notes: No correction required below 1 sec. Kodak's published guidance starts at 1 sec (+1 stop, corrected 2 sec).

Tc = 2.0137 × Tm^P, P ≈ 1.3891. Free log-log least squares fit through Kodak's published corrected-time rows (1 sec → 2 sec, 10 sec → 50 sec, 100 sec → 1200 sec). At the 1 sec boundary the formula intentionally jumps from the no-correction band to ≈ 2 sec, matching Kodak's published step from 0 stops to +1 stop. Inputs above 100 sec continue on the same curve as numeric continuation outside the published source range.

source.title: KODAK PROFESSIONAL TRI-X 400 Film — Technical Data

profiles.name: Official log-log formula fit through published 10/100 sec corrected times
notes: Source data sheet states no exposure adjustment is required from 1/1,000 sec to 1/10 sec.

Source data sheet also lists +1/3 stop at 1/10,000 sec for short-exposure (high-speed) photography; the long-exposure reciprocity formula deliberately excludes that short-exposure anchor — it is preserved as a profile-level note only.
No exposure adjustment required from 1/1000 sec to 1/10 sec per the published data sheet.

Tc = 0.1 × (Tm / 0.1)^P, P ≈ 1.0966. Threshold-anchored constrained log-log fit through the 1/10 sec no-correction boundary and Kodak's published 10 sec → 15 sec / 100 sec → 200 sec corrected-time rows. The 1 sec +1/3 stop row is preserved as source evidence (stop delta only — Kodak publishes no corrected time at 1 sec, so the catalog does not synthesize one) and does not act as a fitting point; the 1/10,000 sec short-exposure guidance is preserved as a profile note and deliberately excluded from the long-exposure curve. Inputs above 100 sec continue on the same curve as numeric continuation outside the published source range.

source.title: KODAK PROFESSIONAL T-MAX 100 Film — Technical Data

notes: 1 sec → +1/3 stop. Source publishes only the stop delta; the derived corrected time (≈ 1.26 sec) tracks the formula curve within ~1/80 stop, so this row is kept as source evidence rather than a fitting point.

profiles.name: Official log-log formula fit through published 10/100 sec corrected times

notes: No adjustment required from 1/10,000 sec to 1 sec.

Tc = Tm^P, P ≈ 1.2261. Threshold-anchored constrained log-log fit through the 1 sec no-correction boundary and Kodak's published 10 sec → 15 sec / 100 sec → 300 sec corrected-time rows. Kodak rounds the published corrected times for practical use, so the curve sits ~1/6 stop above the 10 sec row and ~1/12 stop below the 100 sec row; the markers stay visible as source evidence so users can verify the deviation. Inputs above 100 sec continue on the same curve as numeric continuation outside the published source range.

source.title: KODAK PROFESSIONAL T-MAX 400 Film — Technical Data

source.title: KODAK PROFESSIONAL EKTACHROME E100 Film — Technical Data

profiles.name: Official constant correction across published 120–1000 s range

notes: No correction required for exposures below 120 sec.

Tc = √2 × Tm (constant +1/2 stop). Encodes Fujifilm's published 120–1000 sec range guidance verbatim. Above 1000 sec the same +1/2 stop continues as numeric continuation outside the published source range.

profiles.name: Official formula anchored to 1 s threshold

notes: No correction required from 1/4000 sec to 1 sec.
Tc = Tm^P, P ≈ 1.1821. Threshold-anchored constrained log-log fit through Fujifilm's published 4/8/16/32 sec source references. 64 sec is not recommended.

profiles.name: Official formula anchored to 60 s threshold

notes: No correction required from 1/4000 sec to 1 min.

Tc = 60 × (Tm / 60)^P, P ≈ 1.2667. Threshold-anchored constrained log-log fit through the 60 s no-correction boundary and Fujifilm's published 2-min (+1/3 stop) and 4-min (+1/2 stop) references. Inputs above 240 sec extrapolate the same curve outside the published source range.

profiles.name: Official formula anchored to 240 s reference

notes: No correction required from 1/4000 sec to 128 sec.
Tc = 128 × (Tm / 128)^P, P ≈ 1.3676. Anchored to Fujifilm's published 4-min (240 s) +1/3-stop reference. 8 min is not recommended.

profiles.name: Official log-log formula fit through published multiplier rows

notes: No correction required from 1/1000 sec to 1/2 sec.

Tc = 2.2457 × Tm^P, P ≈ 1.4515. Free log-log fit through FOMA BOHEMIA's published multiplier rows at 1/10/100 sec (×2 / ×8 / ×16). The data sheet rows do not lie on a single power law, so the fit sits ~1/6 stop above the 1 sec and 100 sec rows and ~1/3 stop below the 10 sec row; the source rows stay visible as evidence so the deviation is reviewable. Inputs above 100 sec continue on the same curve as numeric continuation outside the published source range.

profiles.name: Official log-log formula fit through published multiplier rows

notes: No correction required from 1/1000 sec to 1/2 sec.

Tc = 3.2107 × Tm^P, P ≈ 1.3891. Free log-log fit through FOMA BOHEMIA's published multiplier rows at 1/10/100 sec (×3 / ×9 / ×18). Residuals stay within ~1/5 stop at every published row; the source rows stay visible as evidence so the fit is reviewable. Inputs above 100 sec continue on the same curve as numeric continuation outside the published source range.

profiles.name: Official log-log formula fit through published multiplier rows

notes: No correction required from 1/1000 sec to 1/2 sec.

Tc = 1.8022 × Tm^P, P ≈ 1.3635. Free log-log fit through FOMA BOHEMIA's published multiplier rows at 1/10/100 sec (×1.5 / ×6 / ×8). FOMAPAN 400's published rows step ×1.5 → ×6 → ×8 — the 10 sec row sits roughly half a stop above the curve while the 1 and 100 sec rows sit roughly a quarter stop below; the source rows stay visible as evidence so the deviation is reviewable. Inputs above 100 sec continue on the same curve as numeric continuation outside the published source range.

profiles.name: Official log-log formula fit through published corrected-time rows

notes: No correction required from 1/1000 sec to 1 sec.

Tc = 0.9248 × Tm^P, P ≈ 1.4652. Free log-log fit through Rollei's published 2/5/10/20/30 sec corrected-time rows. Rollei's published rows do not lie on a single power law, so residuals reach ~0.3 stop at the 2/5 sec rows; the source rows stay visible as evidence so the deviation is reviewable. Inputs above 30 sec continue on the same curve as numeric continuation outside the published source range.

profiles.name: Official log-log formula fit through published corrected-time rows

notes: No correction required from 1/1000 sec to 1/2 sec.

Tc = 1.7708 × Tm^P, P ≈ 1.2404. Free log-log fit through Rollei's published 1/5/10/15/20 sec corrected-time rows. Residuals reach ~0.4 stop at the 5 sec row where the published step is largest; the source rows stay visible as evidence so the deviation is reviewable. Inputs above 20 sec continue on the same curve as numeric continuation outside the published source range.

profiles.name: Official log-log formula fit through published quantified rows

notes: No correction required from 1/1000 sec to 1/2 sec.

Tc = 0.9601 × Tm^P, P ≈ 1.5361. Free log-log fit through Rollei's four published quantified corrected-time rows (4/8/15/30 sec). The 1 sec and 2 sec published rows give corrected exposure as ranges (1–2 sec and 3–4 sec) and are preserved as source-evidence notes — they are never used as exact fitting points. Residuals at the four quantified rows stay within ~1/30 stop. Inputs above 30 sec continue on the same curve as numeric continuation outside the published source range.

note.text: Effective exposure 1 to 2 sec per published data sheet (range preserved as note).
Effective exposure 3 to 4 sec per published data sheet (range preserved as note).

profiles.name: Official log-log formula fit through published quantified rows

notes: No correction required from 1/1000 sec to 1/2 sec.

Tc = 0.9601 × Tm^P, P ≈ 1.5361. Free log-log fit through Rollei's four published quantified corrected-time rows (4/8/15/30 sec); identical shape to RETRO 80S because Rollei publishes the same correction values for both films. The 1 sec and 2 sec published rows give corrected exposure as ranges (1–2 sec and 3–4 sec) and are preserved as source-evidence notes — they are never used as exact fitting points. Residuals at the four quantified rows stay within ~1/30 stop. Inputs above 30 sec continue on the same curve as numeric continuation outside the published source range.

note.text: Effective exposure 1 to 2 sec per published data sheet (range preserved as note).
Effective exposure 3 to 4 sec per published data sheet (range preserved as note).

profiles.name: Official log-log formula fit through published 2/4/8/15 sec rows

notes: No correction required for exposures up to 1 sec.

Tc = 1.2102 × Tm^P, P ≈ 1.3423. Log-log fit through the four published multiplier rows in ADOX's 2024 CHS 100 II technical sheet (2 / 4 / 8 / 15 sec). The sheet does not publish reciprocity rows past 15 sec; inputs above 15 sec continue on the same curve as numeric continuation outside the published source range.

source.citation: ADOX CHS 100 II technical sheet (11 July 2024)
source.title: ADOX CHS 100 II S/W Film — Technische Beschreibung, 11. Juli 2024

profiles.name: Official log-log formula anchored to 1 s and 10 s reference points

notes: No correction required from 1/1000 sec to 1 sec.

Tc = 1.4142 × Tm^P, P ≈ 1.150515. Log-log fit through ADOX's published anchors at 1 sec (+1/2 stop) and 10 sec (+1 stop). 100 sec is published as 'Not recommended' — inputs at or above 100 sec are unsupported and no corrected exposure is calculated.
1/1000 sec → +1/2 stop. Preserved as published evidence only; the calculation path stays no-correction across the entire sub-1 sec band.
